profile.name: Marcus Reed
profile.headline: The Side-Hustler · 2 flats in East London
profile.bio: Marcus is 41, an operations manager at a logistics firm, and lets two flats on the side, the 1-bed in Hackney he used to live in, plus a studio in Bethnal Green he bought in 2021. Hosting was meant to be passive income; it quietly became a second job on top of a real one. He handles messages between meetings, drives over on weekends when a cleaner cancels, and lies awake before a check-in wondering if the keys were left out. He wants to keep the income without the grind, and above all not to screw it up in front of guests.

sections.title: Jobs to be done
content: Run both flats reliably and professionally without it eating his evenings or his focus at work.
Cover the operation when something slips, a cleaner cancels, a guest messages at midnight, a boiler dies, without dropping everything to drive over.
Give guests a consistent, professional experience so the reviews stay high without him being always-on.
Trade the constant did-I-forget-something hum for reliable oversight and reassurance.

sections.title: Pains
content: Time: check-in days and weekends disappear; he's permanently on call between meetings.
Mental load: both flats live in his head all day as a low background anxiety.
Money: cleaning and turnover costs creep, and he has no clear view of what each flat actually nets.
Risk: a missed clean, a lockout, or a no-show can tank a review while he's stuck at the office.

sections.title: Lifestyle
sections.content: The passive income that became a full-time job he never applied for. Marcus holds down a real career and a family alongside hosting, and is always half-on. He fears losing control and feels quietly chained to the side-hustle he started to buy himself freedom. What he wants isn't more features, it's to feel in control without being involved in every turnover.

sections.title: Value we promise
sections.content: Run both flats like a pro with a fraction of the day-to-day effort. A calm layer of oversight and reassurance, the AI host handles the running, Marcus keeps the control, not another dashboard to check.

prompts.title: Reclaim the evenings
prompts.body:
Run hububb-creative for host.

Angle: The side hustle that quietly took over the evenings. Hububb runs the flats so the host gets the nights back.
Description: Leans on always-on message handling and automated turnovers. Should leave a feeling of relief, the phone stops owning the evening.

Speak to: Marcus Reed, the side-hustler with 2 flats and a full-time job.

Run hububb-creative for host.

Description: Leans on automated cleaning dispatched at checkout. The feeling: the turnover is handled without him driving over.

Speak to: Marcus Reed, the side-hustler who cannot leave work to cover a cancellation.

prompts.title: Answered at midnight
prompts.body:
Run hububb-creative for host.

Angle: Guests message at every hour. The host should not have to be the one awake for it. Hostie answers in his voice, day and night.
Description: Leans on the AI guest messaging agent. The feeling: the mental load lifts, he is no longer the on-call operator.
